def: The town or city where someone lives.
A mobile phone with internet access and apps.
A battery that can be recharged with electricity.
A person who is invited to visit or stay somewhere.
A person who belongs to a club, group, or organization.
A place of higher education where people study advanced subjects.
The parents of someone's mother or father.
The way something moves or points; a course to follow.
To tell someone your name, or to picture something in your mind.
A person who builds and designs websites.
A doctor who treats teeth and gums.
A flat shared by several people who are not family.
A course that covers a lot of material in a short, concentrated time.
The skill of planning and organizing how you use your time.
A group of people working together toward a shared goal.
Causing worry or pressure; hard to deal with calmly.
A child who has no brothers or sisters.
A diagram showing how family members are related across generations.
No longer married, after a legal divorce.
A person connected to you by family or blood.
Communication or connection with another person.
A place with equipment for exercising and working out.
Winning a game, contest, or battle.
The person or team that wins a competition.
To move your body to music, often in a set pattern.
A person who lives in a particular place or building.
Renting a room or flat from someone who is already the tenant.
A room for just one person, as in a dorm or hotel.
A unit of area equal to a square one metre on each side.
A piece of furniture with drawers for storing clothes.
A machine that washes dishes automatically.
A board for pinning up notes, photos, or reminders.
A hard, shiny material like iron, gold, or steel.
A dormitory building where university students live.
